The Leap™ quantum cloud service provides real-time access to the world's largest quantum computers and powerful hybrid solvers while offering production-grade accessibility, reliability, and security.
For D-Wave Quantum, it's not just about developing quantum computing technology, but it's also about optimizing the customer experience.

Access to the technology has been made easy via the development of a point-and-click interface that features multiple quantum-classical hybrid solvers without removing the possibility of programming at other levels of the stack for more advanced developers.

The Leap™ quantum cloud service provides real-time access to the world's largest quantum computers and powerful [hybrid solvers](https://www.dwavequantum.com/solutions-and-products/cloud-platform/hybrid-solver/) while offering production-grade accessibility, reliability, and security.

The Leap service can help customers attain operational efficiencies with access to quantum computing capabilities that address challenges in areas such as workforce and production scheduling, vehicle routing, resource optimization, and cargo loading among others.

The Leap service also provides access to a suite of hybrid solvers that accept industry-scale problems—with up to two million variables and constraints—so customers can address large and complex optimization challenges across logistics, manufacturing and more.

The Leap service can help customers attain operational efficiencies with access to quantum computing capabilities that address challenges in areas such as workforce and production scheduling, vehicle routing, resource optimization, and cargo loading among others.

What's more, the Leap service also provides access to a suite of hybrid solvers that accept industry-scale problems—with up to two million variables and constraints—so customers can address large and complex optimization challenges across logistics, manufacturing and more.
The Leap service also includes demos and other learning resources, as well a vibrant developer community, to help customers get up and running quickly.
D-Wave consistently releases the latest technology through the Leap service as soon as it's ready, as recently evidenced by the Advantage2 prototypes made available prior to full product launches.
Getting started with the Leap service doesn't require a quantum physics degree.
